build.name: build
build.description: Phase 3 of the Medium-path workflow. Use after plan produced PLAN.md. Iterates slices, spawning coder+verifier in chain mode (with retry-on-fail) or multiple coders in parallel for disjoint slices. Handles HITL gates for high-risk paths.
compactRules: Read `PLAN.md` produced by the plan skill. Iterate slices **in the order listed**, respecting `blocks:` markers.
For each slice, iterate its steps in order. One step = one `coder` invocation.
Default mode is `chain`: `coder` â†’ `verifier` per step. Verifier sees only files the coder modified.
If two or more consecutive slices have `parallel: true` AND `blocks:` is empty AND their declared files are disjoint, launch them in `parallel` mode (one `coder` per slice).
Respect `pi-sub-agent` limits: `MAX_PARALLEL_TASKS=8`, `MAX_CONCURRENCY=4`. Queue the rest.
On `coder` `status: success` â†’ the chain step automatically invokes `verifier`. No extra orchestrator action.
On `coder` `status: needs_review` â†’ STOP. Surface `hitl_reason` + `files_changed` to user. Wait for explicit approval before next step.
On `coder` `status: blocked` â†’ STOP. Report `blocker_reason` and last `verifier_feedback` to user.
On `verifier` `status: pass` â†’ mark step done, advance to next step or next slice.
On `verifier` `status: fail` â†’ re-invoke `coder` with `verifier_feedback`. Max **2 retries** per step; third failure â†’ orchestrator marks step `status: blocked` and stops the slice.
On `verifier` `status: error` â†’ distinct from fail. Report tooling issue (timeout, crashed runner, missing dep) to user; do not retry coder.
Pass the user-task verbatim portion of each step to the coder; do NOT re-summarize PLAN content.
Do NOT call `neurox_save` here â€” the orchestrator persists after `validate`.
Do NOT commit, push, or run the full suite manually. Verifier owns gates; production-gate handles publish.

discover.name: discover
discover.description: Phase 1 of the Medium-path workflow. Use when triage classifies a task as medium or substantial. Delegates to the scout sub-agent to gather codebase context + Neurox prior decisions, then returns a structured exploration envelope to be passed to the plan skill.
compactRules: Always invoke the `subagent` tool with `agent: "scout"` and `agentScope: "project"` (or `"both"` if user agents needed too).
Pass the user's task description verbatim in the `task` field; do NOT pre-summarize.
The scout returns an envelope. Parse it but do NOT proceed to plan if `status: blocked` or `status: partial` â€” report the issue back to the user first.
Save key decisions surfaced from Neurox to in-session memory by referencing them in the next phase's prompt. Do NOT call `neurox_save` here.
If the scout's envelope has `open_questions: [...]`, surface them to the user BEFORE running plan. Plan cannot proceed with unresolved ambiguity.
Skip this skill entirely for `conversational` and `small` triage paths.

plan.name: plan
plan.description: Phase 2 of the Medium-path workflow. Use after discover has returned a ready scout envelope. Delegates to the tech-planner sub-agent to produce a prescriptive PLAN.md with vertical slices, then hands off to the build skill.
compactRules: Always invoke the `subagent` tool with `agent: "tech-planner"` and `agentScope: "project"`. Single instance only â€” never parallelize tech-planner.

Pass BOTH inputs in the `task` field: the scout's envelope (verbatim YAML) AND the user's original task description. Do NOT pre-summarize or reshape the envelope â€” tech-planner needs the raw structured data.
Only run this skill if `discover` returned `status: ready` and no unresolved `open_questions`. Otherwise STOP.
The tech-planner returns an envelope with `status: ready | questions_pending | blocked`. Parse the fenced ` ```yaml envelope ` block at the end of its output.
Capture the `artifacts:` path (either `PLAN.md` or `.skynex/<feature>/PLAN.md`) â€” this is the handoff to `build`.
If the envelope lists `new_dependencies: [...]` or any slice has `requires_hitl: true`, surface that to the user BEFORE invoking `build`.
Skip this skill entirely for `conversational` and `small` triage paths.

validate.name: validate
validate.description: Phase 4 of the Medium-path workflow. Runs test-reviewer + security (Ă—2 dual-judge) + skill-validator IN PARALLEL on the slice's changed files, then synthesizes 4 envelopes into one verdict (APPROVED | NEEDS_FIX | ESCALATED).
compactRules: Input is the `changed_files: string[]` from the build envelope. If empty â†’ skip the phase and emit `APPROVED` with `note: "no changes to validate"`.
Always invoke the `subagent` tool with `agentScope: "project"` and `tasks: [...]` (parallel mode, 4 tasks).
Launch ALL FOUR sub-agents in a single `subagent` call. Do NOT serialize them. 4 tasks â‰¤ `MAX_PARALLEL_TASKS=8` and `MAX_CONCURRENCY=4` runs them truly concurrently.
The two `security` instances must receive the **same file list**. Distinguish them only by `(judge 1)` / `(judge 2)` in the task string for trace clarity. They must not know about each other.
Pass the test files subset (filter `changed_files` by `*.test.*` / `*.spec.*` / `__tests__/**`) to `test-reviewer`. Pass the full list to `security` and `skill-validator`.
Parse all four `yaml envelope` fenced blocks. If any envelope fails to parse â†’ status `ESCALATED`, surface the broken envelope verbatim to the user.
Apply the synthesis logic below to produce ONE overall verdict. Do NOT report 4 raw envelopes â€” synthesize.
Do NOT call `neurox_save` here. The orchestrator persists post-synthesis.
Do NOT re-run validate to "break ties" between security judges â€” escalate instead.
If overall verdict is `NEEDS_FIX`, return control to the `build` phase with the union of all blocker findings; do NOT loop here.
